description_long: This object consists of a vertical stack of five irregularly shaped stones, each with rough, angular surfaces and natural contours. The stones vary in size and orientation, with some appearing slightly larger and flatter, while others are more rounded or slanted. The coloration is predominantly a muted grayish-brown, with hints of green moss or lichen visible at the base and in crevices, suggesting some exposure to the elements. The overall structure is uneven, with stones jutting out at different angles, creating a rugged, organic appearance. The surface texture is coarse and granular, with visible chips and natural fractures. This arrangement is typical of decorative or functional stone barriers found in parks, gardens, or along urban walkways.
description: A rugged vertical stack of five irregular, gray-brown stones with rough surfaces and hints of green moss, forming a naturalistic barrier.
description_view_0: A narrow side view showing the vertical alignment of the stacked stones, emphasizing their height and uneven edges.
description_view_1: A top-down or low-angle view revealing the individual stones laid out horizontally, highlighting their varied shapes and surface textures.
description_view_2: A frontal view displaying the full height and staggered arrangement of the stones, with visible moss at the base.
description_view_3: A rear or alternate side view showing the stones' irregular profiles and the way they rest against each other.
attribute_pattern: Irregular mottling and natural fractures, with some patches of green moss; no artificial carvings or regular patterns.
attribute_weathering: Rough with visible chips and patches of moss, indicating moderate weathering and outdoor exposure.
attribute_integration_with_surroundings: Blends naturally with adjacent landscaping stones and low vegetation, partially covered at the base by moss.
